questions.q: Which constitutional office did Sukumar Sen hold as a 'first' in independent India?
options: First Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen was the first Chief Election Commissioner of India.

questions.q: Sukumar Sen organised which landmark democratic exercise?
questions.explanation: He organised India's first general election, held in 1951-52.

questions.q: The Election Commission of India, which Sukumar Sen first led, administers elections to which offices?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission administers elections to Parliament, state legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President.

questions.q: How many general elections did Sukumar Sen oversee as Chief Election Commissioner?
questions.explanation: He oversaw the first two general elections, of 1951-52 and 1957.

questions.q: The second general election conducted under Sukumar Sen was held in which year?
questions.explanation: The second general election under his tenure was held in 1957.

questions.q: Before joining the Election Commission, Sukumar Sen belonged to which service?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen was a member of the Indian Civil Service before becoming Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.q: The first general election that Sukumar Sen organised is notable because most voters were:
questions.explanation: Most of the more than 170 million eligible voters were voting for the first time.

questions.q: The office Sukumar Sen held is headed by an official titled the:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is headed by the Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.q: Sukumar Sen's task in the first election was primarily described as a major exercise in:
questions.explanation: He oversaw the enormous logistical task of conducting elections for a newly independent nation.

questions.q: The first general election Sukumar Sen managed was conducted under which voting principle introduced by the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Constitution introduced universal adult franchise, giving every adult citizen the right to vote.

questions.q: In which year was Sukumar Sen appointed as the first Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: He was appointed the first Chief Election Commissioner in 1950, the year the Election Commission was established.

questions.q: The Election Commission of India, which Sukumar Sen first headed, was established on which date?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India was established on 25 January 1950.

questions.q: Under which Part of the Constitution is the Election Commission, the body Sukumar Sen first led, principally provided for?
questions.explanation: Part XV of the Constitution deals with elections and provides for the Election Commission.

questions.q: Which article of the Constitution provides for the superintendence of elections by the Election Commission that Sen headed?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.q: The first general election supervised by Sukumar Sen had how many eligible voters, approximately?
questions.explanation: More than 170 million voters were eligible in the first general election.

questions.q: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner, the post Sukumar Sen first occupied?
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner is appointed by the President of India.

questions.q: During Sukumar Sen's first election, what method was used widely to help voters identify candidates?
options: Symbols allotted to candidates and parties
questions.explanation: Election symbols were used to help the largely first-time electorate identify candidates and parties.

questions.q: At the time of the first general election, what voting technology was used, before the later adoption of electronic machines?
questions.explanation: The early general elections used paper ballots; Electronic Voting Machines came much later.

questions.q: The Election Commission Sukumar Sen led is best described as which kind of body?
options: A department of the Finance Ministry
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is an autonomous constitutional body.

questions.q: Sukumar Sen's first general election is often described as the largest of its kind in the world at the time because of its:
questions.explanation: The vast scale of the electorate made it the largest democratic exercise in the world at that time.

questions.q: In which region or province of pre-partition India did Sukumar Sen have his administrative roots?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen was an administrator with roots in the Bengal cadre of the civil service.

questions.q: The general elections Sukumar Sen conducted were held under the principle that the voting age was then set at:
questions.explanation: The voting age was 21 at the time; it was lowered to 18 only by the 61st Amendment in 1988.

questions.q: The first general election organised by Sukumar Sen was conducted over which span of months?
questions.explanation: The first general election was held from October 1951 to February 1952.

questions.q: Sukumar Sen, in his role, drew on which 1951 law that governs the conduct of elections in India?
options: The Representation of the People Act
questions.explanation: The Representation of the People Act, 1951 governs the conduct of elections that the Commission administers.

questions.q: Apart from elections in India, Sukumar Sen was invited to help organise the first general election of which neighbouring country?
questions.explanation: After his Indian service, Sukumar Sen was invited to help organise Sudan's first general election.

questions.q: When Sukumar Sen served, the Election Commission was structured as which type of body?
questions.explanation: During Sen's tenure the Commission functioned as a single-member body headed by the Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.q: Sukumar Sen's conduct of the first election required preparing electoral rolls listing voters by which method then required?
options: Enrolling each adult citizen by name
questions.explanation: Preparing rolls under universal adult franchise required enrolling every adult citizen by name.

questions.q: The two general elections conducted by Sukumar Sen used a ballot system in which each candidate or party was associated chiefly with a:
options: Separate ballot box bearing a symbol
questions.explanation: In the early elections, separate ballot boxes marked with party symbols were used to record votes.

questions.q: The constitutional protection of the Chief Election Commissioner's tenure, applicable to Sen's office, links removal to the procedure for removing whom?
options: A judge of the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only in the manner and on grounds similar to a Supreme Court judge.

questions.q: Sukumar Sen's tenure as the first Chief Election Commissioner ran approximately from 1950 until which year?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen served as Chief Election Commissioner from 1950 until around 1958, covering the first two general elections.
